speaker: UNKNOWN
text: making any final adjustments that will help you to feel really cozy.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: And I'm going to lead you through just under 15 minutes of yoga Nidra.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Take an inhale through the nose and let the breath go through the mouth.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Just let the breath drop away.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: We'll do that again, so and inhale through the nose. And then as you exhale, let the weight of the body drop away. Do that one more time.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: And if you have any more sizes that need to come out, just let as many of those size out as there are.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Your arms are welcome, too.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Welcoming yourself to this practice, this protected space of yoga Nidra.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Receiving welcome from the earth beneath you, the earth that holds you, that holds you up.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Noticing the touch of air, the space that holds you,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: the space around you that gets slightly smaller when you inhale, a little bigger when you exhale.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Notice the outline of the body in the space.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Now, let the lower row of teeth drop away from the upper row of teeth with the mouth remaining closed.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Soften your eyes, your eyelids, smooth your brow.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Even soften the skin on the scalp.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: I'm going to take 10 conscious breaths now with the next exhale being 10, and the next exhale being nine.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Just continue to follow the exhales counting down until we reach one.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: If you lose track, just go back to the last number you remember.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Then take your awareness all the way down to your toes, leaving the breath behind for now, just resting your awareness in your toes.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Noticing the feeling of the tips of the toes, and then the toenails, the skin underneath the toenails, the shape of your feet. Notice the inside of the feet. And then moving to the ankles.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Becoming aware of the right ankle and the left ankle.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: And then bringing into the awareness the whole of the right leg.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: And then the whole of the left leg.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Become aware of the weight of the legs, imagining them to be at the same time heavy

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: but also light.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Notice how the legs feel heavy and at the same time light.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: And then letting the legs just dissolve in awareness and bringing your attention to the pelvis.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: The buttocks, the belly,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: the shape of the pelvic bones, these big curved sculpted bones of the pelvis.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: The lower spine.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: And then becoming aware of the belly.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Maybe some sensation here of your breath.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: The internal shifts caused by the movements of the breath.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Tracing the line of the spine from tail to sacrum, to lower spine, just climbing one vertebra at a time along the spine.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: That curved S-shape of the spine, all these little spiny processes, little sticking out bits on the back of the spine and the spongy discs in between.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Become aware now as you move towards the top of the spine,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: of the shoulders, the right shoulder, the left shoulder,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: the finer or elegant bones of the shoulders.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: The complex of muscle.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: The arms, right arm, left arm.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: What does it feel like to have arms?

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: The wrists, the hands.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Notice the shape of the hands.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: The inside of the hands.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: The feeling of the tips of the fingers.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Notice the fingernails.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: The skin underneath the fingernails.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Then tracing your awareness all the way back along the arms into the shoulders, along the neck, into the head.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: And just letting your attention drift just very lightly, very lightly flowing through the skin of the face.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: The awareness resting very softly on the face and all its curves.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: It's contours.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: All its stories.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: The skin of the scalp.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: And the whole body now.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Watching yourself, every single cell alive with prana, life force.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: This vast awareness, expansive.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Now taking a few slightly deeper breaths.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: When you feel ready to, just thinking about moving.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Moving your energy into the room, noticing the sounds you can hear.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: And when you feel ready to,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: just bringing in some gentle movements, those of you who are practicing at home, stay of course for as long as you want to rest.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: And if it's time for you to come back, then we'll be rolling over onto one side and coming all the way up to take a seat.
